summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering the Station 18 fire station replacement project, allocation of remaining ARPA funds for various township improvements, personnel actions including promotions and hires, and discussion of potential state legislation threatening host community cannabis fund revenues. MSP Architects and Conger Construction presented the design development budget for the new Station 18, and the board authorized demolition of the old station and early material procurement.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (July 18 – August 13, 2024) – passed
Motion to proceed with Station 18 demolition and construction and authorize township administrator to sign related documents – passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter Trevor – passed
Motion to approve sale of gear washer and gear dryer from Station 18 – passed
Motion to buy back cemetery plot Section B Lot 467 Grave 7 from Missy Lucan at $500 – passed
Motion to cancel August 27 regular meeting – passed
Motion to appropriate $25,000 from ARPA 2272 to Clermont County Engineer for Deerfield Road/Woodville Pike roundabout improvements – passed
Motion to appropriate $17,475 from ARPA 2272 for service department outbuilding roof replacement by Weatherstop Roofing – passed
Motion to appropriate $24,143 from ARPA 2272 towards purchase of 2023 Ford F-550 plow spreader truck – passed
Motion to approve purchase of 2023 Ford F-550 plow spreader truck from Rush Truck Center for $114,143 – passed
Motion to appropriate $3,267.72 from ARPA 2272 for dais chairs and pass current chairs to police department – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session per ORC 121.22G1 – passed
Motion to appoint Evan Cordray as part-time administrative assistant at $12/hour effective August 24, 2024 – passed
Motion to promote part-time firefighter EMT Shea Newman to full-time at $21/hour effective August 25, 2024 – passed
Motion to promote part-time firefighter EMT Alyssa Malone Ladd to full-time at $21/hour effective August 25, 2024 – passed
Motion to promote part-time firefighter EMT Jack Feagram to full-time at $21/hour effective August 25, 2024 – passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter EMT Jacob Miles at $16/hour effective August 25, 2024 – passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:54 p.m. – passed

notes: Trustee Allen was absent. The board entered executive session at 7:09 p.m. and returned at 7:51 p.m. to discuss personnel matters. The Homorama event begins August 24. The board expressed strong opposition to proposed Ohio state legislation that would redirect host community cannabis tax revenue away from local governments. The Station 18 demolition is expected to begin in September 2024 with a target opening in 2025. This is noted as the first partnership project between Goshen Township and Miami Township. The meeting included a somber acknowledgment of multiple community tragedies including a fatal auto accident and the death of an Indian Hill Service Department worker who helped Goshen after the tornado. ARPA funds must be fully obligated/spent by end of 2025 per federal requirements; Administrator Rose recommends spending all funds in 2024 to avoid compliance issues.
title: BOT 8-13-24
